summary.summary: Adds an explicit sync of pump delivery limits from the user's PumpSettings during pump manager initialization: DeviceDataManager now builds a DeliveryLimits instance using the current pumpSettings.maxBasal and maxBolus (using HKQuantity) and calls pumpManager.syncDeliveryLimits asynchronously, logging failures (commit:bac19bc234c4; diff:848fe584fe35; file:aae13bcd9cb0). Adds DeliveryLimitsSyncTests that verify the maxBasal → maximumBasalRate and maxBolus → maximumBolus mappings and assert user-configured values are preserved (commit:fdcdbc723520; file:e01448936b0f; diff:848fe584fe35). Also bumps the app version to 0.8.3 (commit:ebedcdca96b6; file:355e8efdeed2), updates the OmnipodKit submodule (commit:597774abe907; file:86f289894ab7), blacklists additional older versions (commit:42b6fe606c32; file:c946b13d5f3a), removes an outdated Medtronic note from UI text (commit:a180bd9dfb51; file:1cecd9ffa0b9), and merges the hotfix PR (commit:7b8db13acaac; pr:gordolio/Trio#1219; pr:nightscout/Trio#1219).
summary.userFacingImpact: According to the PR description this hotfix "should solve the `.invalidSetting` failures we've been seeing" (pr:gordolio/Trio#1219; pr:nightscout/Trio#1219). The code change forces the pump manager to receive DeliveryLimits constructed from the user's current PumpSettings at init, which is intended to prevent a stale or default delivery-limits value from causing the pump manager to silently reject temp basals (commit:bac19bc234c4; diff:848fe584fe35). DeliveryLimitsSyncTests were added to guard the mapping and prevent regressions (commit:fdcdbc723520; file:e01448936b0f).
